q:
Who is allowed to cross first at the junction?

A. The brown car.
B. The blue car.
C. The motorcycle.
e:
LESSON 21 – ‘Priority and changing directions’.
The agent gives the order that the brown car and the moto need to stop.

A. The brown car.
B. The blue car.
C. The motorcycle.
clean_e:
LESSON 21 – ‘Priority and changing directions’.
The agent gives the order that the brown car and the moto need to stop.

q:
You approach this junction and you want to cross it.
The oncoming car wants to turn left.
Do you have to give priority?
e:
LESSON 21 – ‘Priority and changing directions’.
If the traffic lights work, the board under the lights has no meaning.
The traffic light is green, so you're allowed to drive straight through.
Your oncoming vehicle (if it also has green light), wants to turn left and must want to give priority.

The traffic light is green, so you're allowed to drive straight through. 
Your oncoming vehicle (if it also has green light), wants to turn left and must want to give priority.

q:
Who is allowed to cross first at the junction?

A. Blue car.
B. Brown car.
C. Motorcycle.
e:
LESSON 21 – ‘Priority and changing directions’.
The agent gives the order that only the moto may drive.
clean_q:
Who is allowed to cross first at the junction?
A. Blue car.
B. Brown car.

clean_e:
LESSON 21 – ‘Priority and changing directions’.
The agent gives the order that only the moto may drive.

q:
What do you know when you see this traffic sign?

A. The indicated road is forbidden for cyclists.
B. By following this road, cyclists may use the carriageway in both directions.
C. It warns drivers who are changing direction that cyclists and riders of two-wheeled mopeds are using the same public road.
e:
LESSON 21
This traffic sign (F50bis) warns drivers who are changing direction that cyclists and riders of two-wheeled mopeds are using the same public road.
When changing direction, you must give way to these road users.
On this sign, sometimes only the cyclist symbol or only a pedestrian crossing symbol may be shown.

What do you know when you see this traffic sign?

clean_e:
LESSON  21

When changing direction, you must give way to these road users.
On this sign, sometimes only the cyclist symbol or only a pedestrian crossing symbol may be shown.

q:
An intersection without traffic signs.
The white car wants to turn left.
The grey car wants to turn right.
Who has the right of way?

A. The white car.
B. The grey car.
C. Whoever turns first automatically has the right of way.
e:
LESSON 21
The grey car is turning right and taking the shorter turn. It has the right of way.
clean_q:
An intersection without traffic signs.
The white car wants to turn left.
The grey car wants to turn right.

A. The white car.
B. The grey car.
C. Whoever turns first automatically has the right of way.
clean_e:
LESSON 21
The grey car is turning right and taking the shorter turn. It has the right of way.

q:
When you carry out a manoeuvre, to whom must you give priority?

A. To drivers only.
B. To all road users.
e:
LESSON 21
A manoeuvre is an action you perform with your vehicle that is not specifically regulated by the traffic rules (for example, reversing).
When you carry out a manoeuvre, you must:
- observe very carefully first,
- give priority to all road users (both drivers and pedestrians).
clean_q:
When you carry out a manoeuvre, to whom must you give priority?
A. To drivers only.
B. To all road users.
clean_e:
LESSON 21
A manoeuvre is an action you perform with your vehicle that is not specifically regulated by the traffic rules (for example, reversing).
When you carry out a manoeuvre, you must:
- observe very carefully first,
- give priority to all road users (both drivers and pedestrians).

q:
In which direction should a car's wheels be turned if the driver wants to turn left, but the intersection is not yet clear?

A. To the left.
B. Straight ahead.
e: If you already turn your wheels to the left and someone hits you from behind, your car may be pushed into the intersection and crash into oncoming vehicles. That’s why your wheels should remain straight until it’s safe to turn.
clean_q:
In which direction should a car's wheels be turned if the driver wants to turn left, but the intersection is not yet clear?
A. To the left.
B. Straight ahead.
clean_e: If you already turn your wheels to the left and someone hits you from behind, your car may be pushed into the intersection and crash into oncoming vehicles. That’s why your wheels should remain straight until it’s safe to turn.

q:
This photo is taken from the car you are driving.
Who has priority?

A. You.
B. The blue car leaving the private driveway of a house.
e:
LESSON 20
The priority to the right rule does not apply when a private driveway joins a public road.
The driver who leaves a private driveway must stop and give way to all road users on the public road (including pedestrians).
That driver is carrying out a manoeuvre.

LESSON 20
The priority to the right rule does not apply when a private driveway joins a public road.
The driver who leaves a private driveway must stop and give way to all road users on the public road (including pedestrians).
That driver is carrying out a manoeuvre.

q:
Who has priority?

A. The red car.
B. The white car.
e:
LESSON 21 – ‘Priority and changing directions’.
The red car who wants to turn right or makes the shortest turn, has priority.
The white car who wants to turn left, or makes the longest turn, has to give priority.
clean_q:
Who has priority? 
A. The red car. 
B. The white car.
clean_e:
LESSON 21 – ‘Priority and changing directions’.
The red car who wants to turn right or makes the shortest turn, has priority.
The white car who wants to turn left, or makes the longest turn, has to give priority.

q:
In what order are these drivers allowed to cross the junction?

A. Blue car – Brown car – Motorcycle.
B. Blue car – Motorcycle – Brown car.
C. Brown car – Blue car – Motorcycle.
e:
LESSON 21 – ‘Priority and changing directions’.
The blue and Brown car have green light.
The driver who wants to drive straight on has priority.
The driver who wants to turn left, has to give priority.
The moto must wait until the traffic light turns green.

q:
In what order are these drivers allowed to cross the junction?

A. Blue car – Brown car – Motorcycle.
B. Blue car – Motorcycle – Brown car.
C. Brown car – Blue car – Motorcycle.
e:
LESSON 21 – ‘Priority and changing directions’.
The blue car and the motorcycle are riding on a priority road.
The blue car want to turn right or makes the short corner and has priority of the motorcycle which makes a long turn.
As soon as the intersection is free, the brown car continue.

q:
A two-way road.
The driver of the white car wants to turn left. He positions himself on the left-hand side of the road.
Is he acting correctly?

A. Yes, this allows following vehicles to pass him on the right.
B. No.
e:
LESSON 21
The road on which the white car is driving is not a one-way street.
- When turning left, the driver positions himself as close as possible to the centre line of the road.
- When turning right, he must position himself as far to the right as possible on the road.
➡️ On a one-way street, a driver who wants to turn left must position himself on the far left side.
clean_q:
A two-way road.
The driver of the white car wants to turn left. He positions himself on the left-hand side of the road.
Is he acting correctly?
A. Yes, this allows following vehicles to pass him on the right.
B. No.
clean_e:
LESSON 21
The road on which the white car is driving is not a one-way street.
- When turning left, the driver positions himself as close as possible to the centre line of the road.
- When turning right, he must position himself as far to the right as possible on the road.
➡️ On a one-way street, a driver who wants to turn left must position himself on the far left side.

q:
How should you position your wheels when you are stopped at an intersection?

A. With the wheels turned in the direction you want to turn.
B. With the wheels pointing straight ahead.
e:
LESSON 21
When you stop at an intersection and intend to turn right, you must keep your wheels straight until you actually begin to turn. This prevents your vehicle from being pushed directly into the side street in case of an unexpected impact, which could create a dangerous situation or force you to give way to oncoming traffic. You only turn when it is safe to do so, taking into account other road users and pedestrians.
clean_q:
How should you position your wheels when you are stopped at an intersection?
A. With the wheels turned in the direction you want to turn.
B. With the wheels pointing straight ahead.
clean_e:
LESSON 21

You only turn when it is safe to do so, taking into account other road users and pedestrians.

q:
Who has priority at this intersection?

A. The tractor driving straight ahead.
B. The car turning left.
e:
LESSON 21
The general rule is that traffic going straight ahead has priority over turning traffic.
However, this rule does not apply when the straight-ahead vehicle is coming from a dirt road.
A driver coming from a dirt road must give way to traffic on the paved road, even when driving straight ahead.

q:
At an intersection you want to turn left.
What do you do?

A. Slow down – Look in the mirror – Turn direction indicator.
B. Turn direction indicator – Look in the mirror – Slow down.
C. Look in the mirror – Turn direction indicator – Slow down.
e:
LESSON 21 – ‘Priority and changing directions’.
See if a car is coming behind you that you for example catches up. Turn Direction indicator on. Slowing down and turn.

q:
The white car wants to reverse into a parking space.
What is correct?

A. The driver must give way to all road users.
B. The driver must give way to all drivers.
C. Approaching drivers must stop and give way to the white car.
e:
LESSON 21
Manoeuvres include:
• changing lanes;
• crossing the carriageway;
• entering a parking space;
• leaving a parking space;
• leaving a property;
• turning around in a street;
• reversing.
When carrying out a manoeuvre, you must:
• do so with great care;
• not endanger any other road users (including pedestrians);
• give way to all road users.
clean_q:
The white car wants to reverse into a parking space.

A. The driver must give way to all road users.
B. The driver must give way to all drivers.
C. Approaching drivers must stop and give way to the white car.

q:
The driver of the blue car wants to turn right.

A. He takes the first right-hand lane (1).
B. He takes the second right-hand lane (2).
C. He can choose between the first or second lane.
e:
LESSON 21 – ‘Priority and changing directions’.
In a build-up area, he can choose the lane he wants to drive on.

q:
You are driving on a one-way road with three lanes and you want to turn left.
Where should you position yourself correctly?

A. In the right lane.
B. In the middle lane.
C. In the left lane.
e:
LESSON 21
When you want to turn left, you must position yourself as far to the left as possible on the roadway.

q:
When there is a problem of the 'dead angle’?

A. Always.
B. Only if the mirrors are incorrectly adjusted.
e:
LESSON 21 – ‘Priority and changing directions’.
Mirrors must be always correctly adjusted before you drive.
But there is always a point that you can't see in the mirrors: the dead angle.
clean_q:

q:
Must you use the left indicator when you're already properly positioned to turn left?

A. Yes, always, and turn it off as soon as the manoeuvre is completed.
B. No, because you're already well positioned.
e:
LESSON 21
A turn signal is there to indicate your intentions, even if you are already in the right position.
